title: ProcedureMetadata status validation error on load

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Error: '1 validation error for ProcedureMetadata status Input should be a valid string' at storage.py:110 on every procedure load.

Root cause: storage.py:116 passes procedure_data.get('status', 'RUNNING') (raw string from GraphQL) to ProcedureMetadata which expects a typed enum for status. The Pydantic error message is misleading — it says 'valid string' but the field is actually an enum.

Fix: Coerce the string to the correct ProcedureStatus enum from tactus.protocols.models before passing to ProcedureMetadata constructor.

File: plexus/cli/procedure/tactus_adapters/storage.py ~line 116.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Investigation update: ProcedureMetadata.status is already typed as str (not an enum) in tactus/protocols/models.py:69. The storage adapter at storage.py:116 passes the raw GraphQL string directly. No coercion is needed and no actual validation error exists in the code.

This issue needs a real repro with an actual error traceback before proceeding. Closing as needs-repro.
